title: podman ps: status always reports "Less than a second ago" for Exited containers
body:
podman ps is always reporting exited containers as having just completed:
```
# podman run fedora date;sleep 10;podman ps -a
Mon Aug  6 16:39:41 UTC 2018
CONTAINER ID   IMAGE                             COMMAND   CREATED          STATUS                              PORTS   NAMES
d78a17bd81ab   docker.io/library/fedora:latest   date      11 seconds ago   Exited (0) Less than a second ago           priceless_payne
```
10 is just an example; even minutes later it still says 'Exited (0) Less than a second ago`.

podman-0.8.1-1.git6b4ab2a.fc28

comments.body: Probably also conmon, since it creates the exit files
Actual exited time is shown in `podman inspect` and seems correct. This seems to just be how `podman ps` displays output.

podman on F28 relies on the installed conmon package.  I just pushed cri-o into testing could you test with the cri-o 1.11 package?

cri-o-1.11.1-1.git1759204.fc28
conmon-1.11.1-1.git1759204.fc28.aarch64.rpm
This one's not conmon - we're not actually calculating exited time properly for both JSON and normal PS output
Closing as the issue has been fixed with https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/1228.
